Name: adaptive_routing_geometric_quantization
Title: Adaptive Geometric Routing for Training-Free Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: Exploring the geometric structure of model representations can enhance the effectiveness of training-free mixed-precision quantization by improving the routing of precision adaptation based on curvature-aware diagnostics.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ and InfoQ have focused on training-free proxies and global information flow for mixed-precision quantization. However, they do not explicitly incorporate a geometric perspective to optimize the routing of precision adaptation. This proposal introduces a curvature-aware approach that can lead to improved performance and understanding of mixed-precision quantization's effects on model representations.
Abstract: This proposal investigates the adaptive routing of precision in mixed-precision quantization through a geometric lens. By leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics of model representations, we aim to develop a training-free framework that dynamically adjusts precision at a granular level. Our approach will utilize deterministic spectral estimators to quantify the geometric properties of the model's subspace and apply these insights to guide the quantization process. This method promises to enhance both the efficiency of mixed-precision schemes and the understanding of their impact on model performance. We will validate our hypothesis through a series of controlled experiments, evaluating the performance of our framework against existing state-of-the-art training-free methods. Our ultimate goal is to provide a deeper understanding of the interplay between model geometry and quantization, paving the way for future advancements in resource-efficient deep learning.
Experiments: Conduct a series of experiments using standard datasets (e.g., ImageNet, COCO) to evaluate the performance of the proposed geometric routing framework compared to traditional mixed-precision quantization methods.
Measure the accuracy and computational efficiency of the model under different geometric routing configurations, analyzing the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on precision adaptation.
Employ ablation studies to isolate the effects of specific geometric features on quantization performance, ensuring that the contributions of the proposed method can be understood in detail.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential challenges in accurately modeling the geometric properties of complex neural network architectures.
The necessity for extensive experimentation to fully understand the implications of geometric routing on various model architectures and data types.
Risk of underperformance compared to existing heuristic methods if the geometric insights do not translate effectively into practical improvements.
